risk_description: Distributed financial transaction inconsistency across Stripe and Wise integrations causing ledger mismatches or lost funds during payout orchestration.
root_cause: Complexity of implementing the Saga pattern across microservices without a dedicated transaction manager or distributed locking mechanism.
mitigation_strategy: Implement rigorous idempotency keys for all payment API calls; Design automated reconciliation jobs (cron) to compare internal ledger vs. Stripe/Wise reports daily; Enforce double-entry accounting principles in the database schema.
contingency_plan: Establish a 'manual intervention queue' for stuck transactions with a predefined budget for covering discrepancies while investigating.
monitoring_trigger: Detection of 'Pending' transaction state exceeding 1 hour or webhook failure rate > 1%.

risk_description: AI Worker hallucination or extraction failure when processing non-standard SOW formats, leading to incorrect project scoping or missed PII redaction.
root_cause: Variability in client document formats (PDF/Docx) and probabilistic nature of LLMs without strict schema enforcement.
mitigation_strategy: Implement 'Human-in-the-Loop' validation UI for Administrators to verify extracted data before finalizing; Use AWS Comprehend for deterministic PII detection prior to LLM processing; Enforce Zod/JSON Schema validation on LLM outputs.
contingency_plan: Fallback to manual data entry workflow if AI confidence score drops below 0.7 for specific document types.
monitoring_trigger: AI extraction confidence scores consistently below threshold or frequent manual overrides by admins.

risk_description: PostgreSQL `pgvector` query latency exceeding 250ms p95 requirement as vendor dataset scales, impacting the responsiveness of the matching engine.
root_cause: Suboptimal indexing strategy (IVFFlat vs HNSW) or lack of read replicas for vector search operations which are CPU intensive.
mitigation_strategy: Implement HNSW (Hierarchical Navigable Small World) indexing for vector columns; separate analytical read queries to a read-replica; implement Redis caching for frequent search queries.
contingency_plan: Migrate vector workload to a dedicated managed service (e.g., Pinecone or OpenSearch) if RDS CPU usage becomes unsustainable.
monitoring_trigger: Database CPU usage > 70% or average query latency > 200ms during load testing.

risk_description: GDPR non-compliance risk due to conflict between 'Immutable Audit Logs' requirement and 'Right to be Forgotten' (Data Erasure).
root_cause: Architectural decision to use write-once storage (OpenSearch) for logs that may inadvertently contain PII referencing user IDs.
mitigation_strategy: Implement 'Crypto-shredding' architecture: store PII encrypted with per-user keys, destroy the key upon deletion request to render immutable log data unreadable; Strictly log only UUIDs, never raw PII.
contingency_plan: Engage legal counsel to define specific data retention exemptions for financial audit logs versus general user data.
monitoring_trigger: Audit logs flagged by PII scanning tools during CI/CD or staging environment checks.

risk_description: Node.js event loop blockage during bulk CSV import of large client/vendor lists, causing API unavailability for other users.
root_cause: CPU-intensive parsing of large CSV files within the main application thread rather than offloading to worker threads or queues.
mitigation_strategy: Mandate use of Streams for CSV parsing to handle memory backpressure; Offload actual processing to BullMQ/SQS background workers; Implement strict file size limits (e.g., 10MB) at the API Gateway level.
contingency_plan: Temporarily disable bulk import feature and require support-ticket based imports via script execution.
monitoring_trigger: API Gateway 504 Gateway Timeouts or Node.js process restarts (OOM) during import operations.

risk_description: Unauthorized access to Project Briefs via Vendor Portal due to token leakage or insufficient validation of short-lived JWTs.
root_cause: Reliance on URL-based tokens which can be leaked in browser history or referrer headers if not handled correctly.
mitigation_strategy: Implement one-time-use tokens (magic links) that exchange for a session cookie upon first access; Enforce strict short expiration (e.g., 15 mins); Log all access attempts with IP correlation.
contingency_plan: Immediate revocation capability for all active portal tokens and forced password reset for affected vendor accounts.
monitoring_trigger: Detecting same token usage from multiple distinct IP addresses or geographic locations.
